title: Persea curranii
intro: Persea curranii, also known as Cebu mahogany, is a plant species in the laurel family (Lauraceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help to alleviate various health issues. The bioactive constituents of Persea curranii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations, including essential oils, extracts, and infusions, often used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Persea curranii is a species of tree in the family Lauraceae. It is native to the Philippines, where it is found growing in the wild. The classification of Persea curranii places it within the genus Persea, which is a group of evergreen trees commonly known as avocado trees. The genus Persea belongs to the order Laurales, and the family Lauraceae is a group of flowering plants often referred to as laurel or avocado family. Persea curranii is closely related to other species within the genus Persea, such as the avocado tree.
